message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Aim</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The aim of this study was to computationally predict conserved RNA sequences and structures known as
                    <jats:italic>cis</jats:italic>
                    -acting RNA elements (CREs) located within the SARS-CoV-2 genome.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Materials & methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Bioinformatics tools were used to analyse and predict
                    <jats:italic>cis</jats:italic>
                    -acting regulatory elements by obtaining viral sequences from available databases.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Computational analysis prediction revealed the presence of RNA stem-loop structures within the 3’ end of the ORF1ab region that are analogous to the previously identified SARS-CoV genomic packaging signals. Alignment-based RNA secondary structures prediction of the 5’ end of the SARS-CoV-2 genome identified also conserved CREs.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>These CREs could be used as potential targets for a vaccine and/or antiviral therapeutics developments; however, further studies would be required to confirm their roles in the SARS-CoV-2 life cycle.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: In silico identification of conserved cis -acting RNA elements in the SARS-CoV-2 genome
